folio.label: 148r
folio.l: What shuln we doon , what shuln we to hi~ seye
Shal it be counseil , seide the first shrewe
And I shal telle it , in wordes fewe
What we shuln don , and brynge it wel a boute
I graunte q¶ that other , out of doute
That be my trouthe , I wol the nat bewreye
Now q¶ the first , thow woost wel we ben tweye
And twoo of vs , shuln strengere ben than on
Loke whan that he is set , that rigút a noon
Arise , as thougú thow woldest , witú hi~ pleye
And I shal ryue hi~ , thurgú the sydes tweye
Whil that thow stroglest witú hi~ , as in game
And witú thy daggere , loke thow do the same
And tha~ne shal al this gold , departed be
My dere frend , be twix me and the
Than moun we bothe , oure lustes alfulfille
And pleye at dys , rigút at oure owne wille
And thus acorded , ben theise shrewes tweye
To slen the thridde , as ©e han herd me seye
¢ This ©ongest , whicú that went to the toun
Ful ofte in herte , he rolletú vp and doun
The beawte of theise Floreyns , newe and brigút
O lord q¶ he , if so were that I mygút
Haue al this tresour , to my self allone
There is noman , that lyuetú vnder the trone
Of god , that shulde lyue so merye as I
And atte the laste , the fend oure enemy
Putte in his thougút , that he shulde poyson beye
Witú whicú he mygút slen , his felawes tweye
For why , the fend fond hi~ , in swicú lyuyngö
That he had leue , hi~ to sorwe bryngö
For this was outrely , his ful entente
To slen he~ bothe , and neuäe to repente
¢ And fortú he gotú , no lengere wold he tarie
In to the toun , vn to a potecarie
And preyed hi~ , that he hi~ wolde selle
Som poison , that he mygút his rattes quelle
And eke there was a Polkat , in his hawe
That as he seide , hise capouns had I slawe
And fayn he wolde wreke hi~ , if he mygút
On vermyn , that destroyed hi~ by nygút
The Potecarie answered , and thow shalt haue
